signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical + Carry
signals.text: Middle East escalation drives safe-haven demand — JPY COT LF net short −65,309 (crowded carry). USD/JPY at 158.95; 160.00 remains the key level that has previously triggered BoJ verbal warnings; intervention risk rises on a sustained break above 160. BoJ survey shows flat loan demand, highlighting fragile Japan growth and cautious outlook. Watch 157.00 as the first technical level on a sustained risk-off move.
evidence: BOJ survey shows flat loan demand

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Vol
signals.text: AUD LF net long +41,675 (CFTC, week ending Apr 14), long%=72.0% — extreme long positioning. HV30 at 6.5%, ATM IV (FXE chain) at 8.2% — options pricing a 1.7 vol-pt premium over realized. AUD/USD at 0.7171; 25d RR at −0.21: mild put skew despite the bullish price trend — market hedging rather than chasing. Watch for LF delta turning negative to invalidate.

signals.title: NZD/USD — Inflation + COT
signals.text: NZ CPI held at 3.1% yoy in Q1, above target, with electricity and petrol driving gains. NZD COT LF net short −17,090, long%=21.7% — short positioning extreme. NZD/USD at 0.5913; RBNZ rate hike expected. Watch 0.6000 as the next resistance level.

signals.title: GBP/USD — CB + Technical
signals.text: BoE 4.50% vs Fed 3.75% — 75bp GBP carry premium supports the pair structurally. GBP LF net long +28,426, AM constructive — positioning aligned with the carry read. GBP/USD at 1.3521; 1.3600 is the next resistance on a continuation. ATM IV 9.5% near HV, 25d RR flat — options neutral.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Positioning
signals.text: EUR LF net short −72, long%=50.0% — flat positioning. EUR/USD at 1.1781; VIX at 18.9 (normal volatility) — cross-asset stress contained. Watch for COT positioning to become extreme to generate a signal.
